meta.name: Mixed Vegetable Sabzi
meta.subtitle: Quick North-Indian-style dry curry with flexible vegetables + masala

source.name: SSA canonical cuisines/indian/recipes/vegetable_sabzi.json
notes: Deterministic stovetop sabzi (dry-ish vegetable curry) designed for flexible pantry vegetables.
Built around cumin bloom + onion-tomato masala + finishing garam masala.
Works as a weeknight main/side; pairs well with basmati rice or naan.

summary: A fast, flexible mixed vegetable sabzi (dry curry) with a classic cumin-onion-tomato masala. Use whatever vegetables you have; finish with garam masala and lemon for brightness.

yield.notes: Serves 4 as a side or 3–4 as a light main with rice/naan.

optional.label: Electric pressure cooker
optional.notes: Not required; sabzi is best sautéed/steam-finished. Use only for potatoes if desired.

notes: Naturally vegetarian; vegan if cooked in oil (no ghee).

ingredients.label: Neutral oil (or ghee)
notes: Use ghee for richer flavor (not vegan).
Mustard oil works for a sharper, more traditional profile (heat until smoking, then cool slightly before spices).

ingredients.label: Cumin seeds
ingredients.notes: If using ground cumin, add later with other ground spices to avoid scorching.

ingredients.label: Mustard seeds (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds a South-Indian tempering vibe; skip for a simpler North-Indian profile.

ingredients.label: Green chili, minced (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste; omit for mild.

ingredients.label: Tomatoes, diced (or crushed)
ingredients.notes: Canned diced/crushed tomatoes are fine; reduce a bit longer to remove raw acidity.

ingredients.label: Kashmiri chili powder (or mild chili powder)
ingredients.notes: Use more for heat or substitute paprika + pinch cayenne.

ingredients.label: Garam masala
ingredients.notes: Add near the end to keep aroma fresh.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust based on salt type and tomato acidity.

ingredients.label: Potatoes, peeled and diced (1/2-inch)
ingredients.notes: Optional but common; provides body. If omitted, reduce steam time.

ingredients.label: Green peas (fresh or frozen)
ingredients.notes: Add near the end so they stay bright.

ingredients.label: Water
ingredients.notes: Used to steam-finish vegetables; add more only if pan is drying out.

ingredients.label: Lemon juice (or amchur)
notes: Adds brightness and balance. If using amchur, start with 1/2 tsp and adjust.

steps.title: Prep vegetables and aromatics
instructions: Dice potatoes (if using) and chop cauliflower, carrots, and green beans into similar-size pieces for even cooking.
Chop onion and mince/grate ginger and garlic. Keep spices measured and ready.
checks: Uniform vegetable size prevents mushy pieces alongside undercooked ones.

steps.title: Bloom whole spices (tadka)
instructions: Heat oil (or ghee) in a skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add cumin seeds (and mustard seeds if using). Let sizzle 15–25 seconds until fragrant and lightly popping.
checks: If spices darken immediately, reduce heat—burnt spices turn bitter.

steps.title: Build the onion base
instructions: Add chopped onion and cook 5–7 minutes, stirring, until softened and lightly golden at the edges.
Add ginger, garlic, and green chili (optional). Cook 45–60 seconds until fragrant.
checks: Cook aromatics just until fragrant—do not brown garlic too hard.

steps.title: Add tomatoes and ground spices (onion-tomato masala)
instructions: Add turmeric, coriander, Kashmiri chili powder, and salt.
Cook 5–8 minutes, stirring, until tomatoes break down and the mixture thickens; oil may separate slightly at the edges.
checks: Masala should lose raw tomato smell and become deeper and sweeter.
If it sticks, add 1–2 tbsp water and scrape the pan (deglaze).

steps.title: Add vegetables and steam-finish
instructions: Add potatoes (if using), cauliflower, carrots, and green beans. Stir to coat in masala.
Add 1/4 cup water, cover with a lid, and cook 10–14 minutes, stirring once or twice, until vegetables are tender-crisp.
If using peas, add them for the last 2–3 minutes.
checks: Potatoes should be fork-tender; cauliflower should hold shape.
If pan dries out before vegetables cook, add 2–3 tbsp water and re-cover.

steps.title: Finish with garam masala and lemon
instructions: Stir in garam masala and cook 30–45 seconds to bloom aroma without scorching.
Optional: add lemon juice (or amchur) to brighten.
Taste and adjust salt and heat.
checks: Add garam masala late—if cooked too long it loses fragrance.

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Serve hot with basmati rice, naan, or roti. Add a cooling side like raita or cucumber salad if desired.

notes: Sabzi is a perfect plate component alongside dal or a meat curry.
For a fuller meal, pair with dal + rice and use sabzi as the vegetable side.
Cool completely and store airtight.
Reheat gently with a splash of water in a covered skillet or microwave covered.
Freezing is possible, but potatoes soften and can become grainy.
If you plan to freeze, consider omitting potatoes and using more cauliflower/beans/peas.
Cool within 2 hours and refrigerate promptly.
Reheat to steaming hot; add a splash of water to prevent drying.
Use 5–6 cups total vegetables. Aim for a mix of: firm (potato/sweet potato), medium (cauliflower/broccoli/carrots), quick (peas/spinach).
Add firm veg first; add quick veg at the end to avoid mush.
swapIdeas: Add bell pepper strips for sweetness.
Add spinach at the end until wilted (2 minutes).
Add zucchini near the end (3–4 minutes) to prevent watery sabzi.

notes: Use mustard seeds + cumin seeds at start.
Add 8–10 curry leaves during tadka.
Optional: add 1/4 tsp hing (asafoetida) if available.
Skip tomatoes; add 2 tbsp yogurt (or coconut milk for vegan) after onions.
Add 1 tsp amchur or 1 tbsp lemon at the end for acidity.
Add 1.5 cups cooked chickpeas with vegetables (great vegan).
Or add 8 oz paneer cubes near the end, seared separately if desired.
Prep vegetables ahead for a 20-minute cook.
Use frozen cauliflower/peas to reduce prep time.
